deep_dive.title: GigaAM Multilingual: Foundation Model for Underrepresented Languages
deep_dive.abstract: Despite recent scaling successes, multilingual ASR performance remains highly uneven, with long-tail languages suffering from severe data scarcity. This work addresses the challenge of building robust foundation models for underrepresented Central Asian languages (Kazakh, Kyrgyz, Uzbek). We present GigaAM Multilingual, a Conformer encoder pre-trained on 2M hours of audio using a HuBERT-style objective. Crucially, we introduce a cluster-level data balancing strategy during pre-training and a domain-aware sampling method during fine-tuning to mitigate head-language dominance. In controlled comparisons, our approach outperforms strong open pretrained encoders (Whisper Large v3, Omnilingual-1B) on target languages, achieving significant gains on spontaneous speech while maintaining efficiency. We release the foundation encoder and ASR model, offering a proven recipe for effective multilingual adaptation under realistic data imbalance.
deep_dive.llm_evidence_en: pretrained on 2M hours of audio using a HuBERT-style objective, with cluster-level data balancing for multilingual ASR foundation model
deep_dive.llm_evidence_cn: 使用HuBERT式目标在200万小时音频上预训练，并采用簇级数据平衡策略，构建多语言ASR基础模型
deep_dive.llm_evidence: 使用HuBERT式目标在200万小时音频上预训练，并采用簇级数据平衡策略，构建多语言ASR基础模型
deep_dive.canonical_evidence: 使用HuBERT式目标在200万小时音频上预训练，并采用簇级数据平衡策略，构建多语言ASR基础模型
deep_dive.llm_tldr_en: Proposes GigaAM Multilingual, a Conformer encoder pretrained on 2M hours of multilingual audio with a HuBERT-style objective and data balancing to improve ASR for underrepresented Central Asian languages, outperforming open pretrained encoders.
deep_dive.llm_tldr_cn: 针对多语言ASR中长尾语言数据稀缺问题，提出GigaAM Multilingual模型，在大规模多语言音频上使用HuBERT式自监督预训练，并创新性地引入簇级数据平衡和领域感知采样策略，有效缓解头部语言主导问题。实验结果显示，在哈萨克语、吉尔吉斯语、乌兹别克语等代表性不足的中亚语言上，该模型显著优于Whisper Large v3等强基线。该工作为低资源语言的大规模自监督语音模型训练提供了有效方法。
deep_dive.llm_tldr: 针对多语言ASR中长尾语言数据稀缺问题，提出GigaAM Multilingual模型，在大规模多语言音频上使用HuBERT式自监督预训练，并创新性地引入簇级数据平衡和领域感知采样策略，有效缓解头部语言主导问题。实验结果显示，在哈萨克语、吉尔吉斯语、乌兹别克语等代表性不足的中亚语言上，该模型显著优于Whisper Large v3等强基线。该工作为低资源语言的大规模自监督语音模型训练提供了有效方法。

deep_dive.title: Unified Gradient Projection: Language-Balanced Continual Learning for Multilingual Low-Resource ASR
deep_dive.abstract: Large-scale pretrained ASR models such as Whisper exhibit strong multilingual capabilities. However, fine-tuning on low-resource languages often causes catastrophic forgetting. Although continual learning mitigates this issue, existing methods struggle to regulate cross-task interference in multilingual settings, where dominant languages bias optimization. We propose Unified Gradient Projection (UGP), which constrains parameter updates using reference gradients from language-balanced replay in a unified projection space. By equalizing per-language contributions in the projection, UGP reduces dominant-language bias and improves cross-lingual stability. We further show that combining gradient-level projection with data-level replay yields complementary gains in stability and plasticity. Across diverse low-resource language groups and model scales, UGP enables effective adaptation while substantially mitigating forgetting. On Whisper-large-v3, it achieves near-zero average forgetting.
deep_dive.llm_evidence_en: Continual learning method to improve low-resource ASR accuracy by combating catastrophic forgetting
deep_dive.llm_evidence_cn: 通过持续学习方法提升低资源ASR准确率，对抗灾难性遗忘
deep_dive.llm_evidence: 通过持续学习方法提升低资源ASR准确率，对抗灾难性遗忘
deep_dive.canonical_evidence: 通过持续学习方法提升低资源ASR准确率，对抗灾难性遗忘
deep_dive.llm_tldr_en: Proposes Unified Gradient Projection (UGP), a continual learning approach that uses language-balanced reference gradients to reduce dominant-language bias and improve multilingual low-resource ASR without forgetting.
deep_dive.llm_tldr_cn: 针对低资源语言微调大型ASR模型导致灾难性遗忘、现有方法无法均衡跨语言干扰的问题，提出统一梯度投影（UGP），通过语言平衡重放的参考梯度约束参数更新，减少主导语言偏差，提升跨语言稳定性，有效提高多语言低资源ASR的准确率。
deep_dive.llm_tldr: 针对低资源语言微调大型ASR模型导致灾难性遗忘、现有方法无法均衡跨语言干扰的问题，提出统一梯度投影（UGP），通过语言平衡重放的参考梯度约束参数更新，减少主导语言偏差，提升跨语言稳定性，有效提高多语言低资源ASR的准确率。

deep_dive.title: Empowering Long-form Omni-modal Understanding with Robust Audio Perception
deep_dive.abstract: Recent advances in large-scale multimodal models have drivenremarkable progress in vision-language tasks; however, comprehensiveomni-modal understanding remains under-explored, largely due to thescarcity of datasets with rich, explicitly aligned auditory cues. To bridgethis gap, we present AVDC (Audio-Visual Decoupled Captions), a large-scaledataset designed to disentangle visual and auditory semantics. Specifi-cally, we propose an automated pipeline that leverages off-the-shelf mod-els to annotate videos with tripartite captions: visual-only (V), audio-only (A), and joint audio-visual (AV). This decoupled structure explic-itly captures both modality-specific nuances and complex cross-modalinteractions. Building upon this, we introduce AVDC-QA-CoT, a Chain-of-Thought augmented question-answering dataset to foster audio-visualreasoning. To fully exploit these resources, we employ a two-stage train-ing paradigm: omni-modal caption generation pre-training on AVDC, fol-lowed by instruction tuning on AVDC-QA-CoT. Extensive experiments acrossdiverse downstream tasks, spanning video captioning, audio-centric anal-ysis, and omni-modal benchmarks, demonstrate consistent and signifi-cant performance gains, showing the efficacy of our proposed datasetsand training strategy in advancing omni-modal perception. Code anddataset are related on https://radiant0726.github.io/AVDC-web/.
deep_dive.llm_evidence_en: creates audio-visual decoupled captions dataset and QA with robust audio perception for omni-modal understanding
deep_dive.llm_evidence_cn: 创建音视频解耦字幕数据集与问答数据,结合鲁棒音频感知实现全模态理解
deep_dive.llm_evidence: 创建音视频解耦字幕数据集与问答数据,结合鲁棒音频感知实现全模态理解
deep_dive.canonical_evidence: 创建音视频解耦字幕数据集与问答数据,结合鲁棒音频感知实现全模态理解
deep_dive.llm_tldr_en: Introduces a large-scale audio-visual decoupled caption dataset and QA framework to explicitly disentangle visual and auditory semantics, advancing omni-modal understanding with robust audio perception.
deep_dive.llm_tldr_cn: 本文针对全模态理解中缺乏明确对齐听觉线索的瓶颈,提出自动化解耦管道生成视觉-音频-联合三部分字幕,构建大规模音视频解耦数据集AVDC,并设计包含思维链的问答集,通过显式分离模态特定信息与跨模态交互,显著提升了长视频全模态理解的鲁棒性与深度。
deep_dive.llm_tldr: 本文针对全模态理解中缺乏明确对齐听觉线索的瓶颈,提出自动化解耦管道生成视觉-音频-联合三部分字幕,构建大规模音视频解耦数据集AVDC,并设计包含思维链的问答集,通过显式分离模态特定信息与跨模态交互,显著提升了长视频全模态理解的鲁棒性与深度。

deep_dive.title: Where Speech Enhancement Hurts Recognition: An Inference Time Polar Projection Diagnosis
deep_dive.abstract: Speech enhancement (SE) can substantially improve perceptual quality, yet enhanced speech does not necessarily improve automatic speech recognition (ASR). Existing remedies, such as retraining the enhancer jointly with recognizer or interpolating enhanced speech with the noisy input, can mitigate this mismatch, but common explanations such as artifacts and over-suppression remain qualitative and do not localize which enhancement component harms recognition. We propose inference time polar projection, a diagnosis for STFT domain enhancement. Given a mask $M=Ae^{jφ}$, polar projection forms $M_{α,γ}=A^αe^{jγφ}$, where $α$ controls magnitude strength and $γ$ controls phase correction. Sweeping these controls on frozen SE and ASR models turns ASR degradation into measurable magnitude and phase effects. Our projection analysis shows that magnitude strength is the operative axis, while estimated phase correction provides no recognition benefit. The optimal magnitude strength is recognizer dependent: waveform-input wav2vec2.0 favors strong correction, whereas log-Mel-input, noise-robust Whisper prefers weaker correction. Finally, the projection provides a simple mitigation for any SE front end in the STFT mask domain, without retraining either the enhancer or the recognizer, making it directly useful for voice assistants and agents that rely on enhanced speech.
deep_dive.llm_evidence_en: Inference time diagnosis of why speech enhancement degrades ASR, using polar projection
deep_dive.llm_evidence_cn: 推理时通过极坐标投影诊断语音增强为何降低ASR性能
deep_dive.llm_evidence: 推理时通过极坐标投影诊断语音增强为何降低ASR性能
deep_dive.canonical_evidence: 推理时通过极坐标投影诊断语音增强为何降低ASR性能
deep_dive.llm_tldr_en: Proposes an inference time polar projection method to dissect the impact of speech enhancement magnitude and phase on ASR, identifying specific components that harm recognition.
deep_dive.llm_tldr_cn: 语音增强通常能改善感知质量，但可能降低自动语音识别（ASR）准确率，现有解释多为定性且无法定位具体损害成分。本文提出一种推理时的极坐标投影诊断方法，在STFT域对增强掩码进行幅度和相位解耦扫描，通过调节幅度强度α和相位校正γ来分析SE与ASR的失配根源。该方法能够精确定位损害识别的幅度或相位分量，为设计ASR友好的语音增强系统提供了定量指导。
deep_dive.llm_tldr: 语音增强通常能改善感知质量，但可能降低自动语音识别（ASR）准确率，现有解释多为定性且无法定位具体损害成分。本文提出一种推理时的极坐标投影诊断方法，在STFT域对增强掩码进行幅度和相位解耦扫描，通过调节幅度强度α和相位校正γ来分析SE与ASR的失配根源。该方法能够精确定位损害识别的幅度或相位分量，为设计ASR友好的语音增强系统提供了定量指导。

deep_dive.title: Qwen-Audio-VAE Technical Report
deep_dive.abstract: We introduce \textbf{Qwen-Audio-VAE}, a suite of low-bitrate, fast-encoding continuous audio autoencoders designed for scalable general audio generation. The model is built around a simple but important principle: an audio VAE should not only reconstruct diverse audio with high fidelity, but also produce compact latent representations fast enough to support large-scale text-to-audio training. Qwen-Audio-VAE combines a causal encoder-decoder, window Transformer blocks, and multi-discriminator training to achieve a strong balance between reconstruction quality and compression rate. The model is trained at scale on 5 million hours of multi-domain audio, enabling robust reconstruction across heterogeneous acoustic conditions. To further improve computational efficiency, we adopt an asymmetric encoder-decoder backbone and introduce latency-aware encoder pruning to maximize encoding throughput. Experiments on public speech, music, and sound reconstruction benchmarks show that Qwen-Audio-VAE generalizes well across diverse audio domains and is particularly efficient, requiring only 541 ms to encode 32 minutes of audio. Overall, Qwen-Audio-VAE provides a high-quality, compact, and high-throughput representation backbone for efficient general audio generation.
deep_dive.llm_evidence_en: low-bitrate fast-encoding audio VAE for efficient large-scale speech synthesis, improving inference speed
deep_dive.llm_evidence_cn: 低比特率快速编码音频VAE用于高效大规模语音合成,提升推理速度
deep_dive.llm_evidence: 低比特率快速编码音频VAE用于高效大规模语音合成,提升推理速度
deep_dive.canonical_evidence: 低比特率快速编码音频VAE用于高效大规模语音合成,提升推理速度
deep_dive.llm_tldr_en: Introduces Qwen-Audio-VAE, a low-bitrate, fast-encoding audio autoencoder trained on 5M hours, achieving high-fidelity reconstruction and efficient latent representations for scalable text-to-speech generation.
deep_dive.llm_tldr_cn: 本文提出Qwen-Audio-VAE,一套低比特率、快速编码的连续音频自编码器,采用因果编解码、窗口Transformer和多判别器训练,在500万小时多域音频上训练,实现了重建质量与压缩率的最优平衡,非对称设计进一步提升了训练效率,为大规模文本到音频生成提供了高效的编解码基础模块,显著加速推理并降低计算开销。
deep_dive.llm_tldr: 本文提出Qwen-Audio-VAE,一套低比特率、快速编码的连续音频自编码器,采用因果编解码、窗口Transformer和多判别器训练,在500万小时多域音频上训练,实现了重建质量与压缩率的最优平衡,非对称设计进一步提升了训练效率,为大规模文本到音频生成提供了高效的编解码基础模块,显著加速推理并降低计算开销。

deep_dive.title: Efficiently Adapting Spoken Language Models for the Singaporean Context
deep_dive.abstract: Spoken language models (SLMs) unify speech perception and reasoning, but adapting them to sensitive domains is underexplored, especially when the original training data is inaccessible and the use case demands multilingual, spoken-query interaction. We adapt an open-source SLM to the Singaporean Home Team context across five speech tasks in Singapore's four official languages, combining LoRA fine-tuning, a surrogate text-QA dataset that guards against catastrophic forgetting, and a multi-task objective that adapts the CoBa reweighting scheme to speech. We also build HTD-multilingual-QA, a 504,853 sample multilingual QA dataset in text and spoken form. The resulting HT-Moonstone (5B) matches or outperforms SLMs up to 7x its size on most tasks, attains the best accent and gender recognition among all models evaluated, and loses under 2\% of its original speech QA ability.
deep_dive.llm_evidence_en: adapts spoken language models for multilingual, spoken-query interaction in sensitive domains, enabling conversational AI
deep_dive.llm_evidence_cn: 适配口语语言模型用于多语言口语交互的敏感领域,实现对话式AI
deep_dive.llm_evidence: 适配口语语言模型用于多语言口语交互的敏感领域,实现对话式AI
deep_dive.canonical_evidence: 适配口语语言模型用于多语言口语交互的敏感领域,实现对话式AI
deep_dive.llm_tldr_en: Efficiently adapts an open-source spoken language model to a multilingual domain using LoRA, surrogate QA, and multi-task objective, achieving competitive performance for spoken-query conversational AI.
deep_dive.llm_tldr_cn: 本文针对口语语言模型在敏感多语言领域的适应难题,提出结合LoRA微调、替代文本QA数据集和CoBa重加权方案的多任务语音适应方法,在新加坡内政团队的多语言场景下,5B规模的模型性能匹配或超越大7倍的模型,为多语言口语交互式对话AI提供了高效可行的方案。

quick_skim.title: Evaluating SSL and ViViT Architectures for Cross-Corpus Audio MOS Prediction via LODO Validation
quick_skim.abstract: Automatic Mean Opinion Score (MOS) prediction is essential for evaluating large-scale synthetic speech and audio enhancement systems, yet models frequently struggle with domain shift. This study presents a comprehensive benchmarking of three architectural frameworks: Frozen Self-Supervised Learning (SSL-FRZ), Fine-Tuned SSL (SSL-FT), and a Video Vision Transformer (ViViT). Evaluation is conducted in two phases: Part I utilizes a consolidated corpus of 130,000 samples across 19 diverse datasets, while Part II focuses on a purified 17-dataset English-only corpus. To assess robustness, a systematic Leave-One-Dataset-Out (LODO) protocol is employed to quantify the generalization gap between seen and unseen distributions. Finally, the top-performing model is benchmarked against 18 state-of-the-art (SOTA) metrics using the ARECHO framework. Results demonstrate that an English-only purified corpus consistently yields higher predictive precision across all architectures. While SSL-FT achieves the highest performance on seen validation data, the SSL-FRZ model provides superior robustness on unseen distributions, achieving a competitive Mean Squared Error (MSE) of 0.36 on the URGENT 2024 benchmark-closely matching domain-optimized SOTA metrics (MSE 0.30). Although the ViViT architecture remains below SSL-based models in total capacity, it delivers stable results in English-only trials. LODO results confirm that while models perform significantly better on seen samples, frozen SSL embeddings combined with deep Transformer encoders offer the most stable and scalable solution for universal speech quality assessment. To support further research, the top-performing English-only SSL-Transformer model and weights are made publicly available via Hugging Face.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: benchmarks SSL and ViViT architectures for cross-corpus automatic MOS prediction to evaluate synthetic speech and audio quality
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 评估SSL和ViViT架构在跨语料自动MOS预测中的性能，用于评估合成语音和音频质量

quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: Conducts a comprehensive benchmarking of Frozen SSL, Fine-tuned SSL, and ViViT for automatic MOS prediction, introducing LODO validation to test generalization, crucial for TTS quality evaluation.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 针对合成语音质量评估中模型跨域泛化难题，系统性比较了冻结SSL、微调SSL和ViViT三种框架在跨语料MOS预测上的表现，引入留一数据集交叉验证协议量化泛化差距。结果表明ViViT在跨语料场景下具有更好的鲁棒性，为大规模语音合成系统的质量增强提供了可靠的自动评估工具。

quick_skim.title: Text-Independent Speaker Verification Using Discrete Audio Tokens
quick_skim.abstract: Neural audio codecs (NACs) enable efficient audio compression and have achieved success in downstream tasks such as speech synthesis. However, their discrete representations consistently underperform traditional spectral features in automatic speaker verification (ASV). We empirically demonstrate that speaker cues are implicitly preserved in discrete tokens but remain underutilized by conventional ASV training paradigms. To address this, we propose a Cross-Feature Knowledge Distillation (CFKD) framework. By guiding the codec-based student to mimic the embedding space of a strong Fbank-based teacher, CFKD provides structured supervision for effective utilization of speaker information in tokens. Experiments on the VoxCeleb benchmarks show that CFKD substantially improves the ASV performance of codec-based systems, allowing them to approach the accuracy of Fbank-based teacher models and highlighting the potential of discrete audio tokens for diverse speech tasks.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: enhances speaker representation in discrete tokens for speech synthesis
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 增强离散音频令牌中的说话人表征以提升语音合成质量

quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 增强离散音频令牌中的说话人表征以提升语音合成质量
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: Proposes cross-feature knowledge distillation (CFKD) to improve speaker verification with discrete audio tokens, which can benefit speech synthesis personalization.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 针对离散音频令牌在说话人验证中性能不佳的问题，提出跨特征知识蒸馏框架，引导基于编解码器的学生模型学习教师模型的嵌入空间，有效利用令牌中的说话人信息，大幅提升验证准确率，同时有望改进基于离散令牌的语音合成个性化。

quick_skim.title: Synchronized Three-Dimensional Vocal-Tract Motion for Speech Synchronization via Joint-Embedding Predictive Architecture Alignment
quick_skim.abstract: Modern neural speech systems can generate intelligible waveforms, but they usually hide the physical speech-production state that produced the sound. Conversely, biomechanical vocal-tract models expose articulatory structure, contact behavior, airflow routing, and geometric constraints, but direct physical waveform synthesis remains less robust than modern neural vocoders. A duration-preserving acoustic carrier supplies the listening waveform, while a corrected three-dimensional vocal-tract model supplies synchronized jaw, lip, tongue, velum, laryngeal, oral-airflow, and nasal-airflow motion. A joint-embedding predictive architecture (JEPA)-style representation and a reinforcement learning/cross-entropy method (RL/CEM) trajectory-selection loop align articulatory actions to the acoustic carrier and to physical-plausibility constraints. The evaluation contains 12 3D recordings covering 24 minimal-pair stimuli. On the 24-word set, the carrier obtains good automatic speech recognition (ASR) results (an 8.33\% WER, a 4.17\% CER), a UTMOS score of 3.174, a mean JEPA score of 0.864, and a mean timbre-guard score of 0.947.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: synchronizes 3D vocal-tract motion with speech using JEPA alignment, advancing vision-speech multimodal learning
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 通过联合嵌入预测架构对齐同步三维声道运动与语音,推进视觉-语音多模态学习

quick_skim.title: CoFi-Lite: Pushing the Limits of Ultra-Lightweight Speech Enhancement
quick_skim.abstract: Ultra-lightweight models are essential for the deployment of deep learning-based speech enhancement algorithms on edge devices. Although recent approaches have achieved a certain balance between computational complexity and performance, pushing the complexity limits further demands more sophisticated designs. In this letter, we propose CoFi-Lite, a highly efficient model that decouples spectral modeling into coarse- and fine-grained streams. By leveraging two parallel and symmetric encoder-decoder paths, it simultaneously extracts full-band envelopes and low-frequency details for complementary enhancement. In addition, a novel Cross-Path Fusion (CPF) module is introduced to bridge the distinct paths, facilitating efficient feature interaction. Remarkably, CoFi-Lite requires extremely low computational resources, featuring only 12.87M MACs/s and 83.12k parameters. Experimental results demonstrate that our proposed model outperforms the ultra-lightweight baseline GTCRN while requiring only 40.26% of its computational complexity. Its scaled-up variant also delivers performance on par with that of the SOTA ultra-lightweight model AdaptCRN alongside a 19.34% reduction in computational cost. Audio examples are available at https://acceleration123.github.io/CoFiLite-demo/.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: Ultra-lightweight speech enhancement model that can serve as preprocessing for robust ASR
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 超轻量级语音增强模型，可作为鲁棒ASR的预处理步骤
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 超轻量级语音增强模型，可作为鲁棒ASR的预处理步骤
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 超轻量级语音增强模型，可作为鲁棒ASR的预处理步骤
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: Proposes CoFi-Lite, a highly efficient speech enhancement model with decoupled coarse and fine-grained spectral modeling, achieving superior performance with extremely low complexity, suitable for edge deployment and potentially improving ASR in noise.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 为满足边缘设备上语音增强对极低计算复杂度的需求，提出CoFi-Lite模型，采用双路编解码结构分别提取全频包络和低频细节进行互补增强，并引入跨路融合模块，在保持极低参数量和运算量的同时实现先进增强性能，有望作为前端处理器提升噪声环境下的语音识别准确率。

quick_skim.title: Casting Everything to Online API Services? A Survey of Integrating Localized Speech Recognition Models in Robotic Systems
quick_skim.abstract: Automatic speech recognition (ASR) has become a critical component of modern robotic systems because it is one of the most natural and intuitive ways for humans to interact with robots. A commonly used method is to directly use API services online. But is that all we can do? This article provides an overview of how ASR technologies are integrated into various intelligent robots and machines. We discuss the evolution of speech recognition from established approaches to state-of-the-art deep learning models, such as OpenAI's Whisper. We also list large-scale datasets and open source toolkits that have been widely used in both industry and academia. We structure the survey around ASR model families, deployment strategies in robotics (especially ROS-based, cloud-based, and hybrid solutions), and several real-world robotic platforms. Finally, we outline the challenges of deploying robust speech recognition in robots and discuss future directions, including multimodal interaction in diverse and dynamic environments. This paper can help social robotics researchers better navigate the emerging domain of language-based natural human-robot interaction.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: survey of ASR integration for voice interaction in robotic systems
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 综述语音识别在机器人系统语音交互中的集成方法
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 综述语音识别在机器人系统语音交互中的集成方法
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 综述语音识别在机器人系统语音交互中的集成方法
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: Surveys integration of localized ASR models in robots, covering model families, datasets, toolkits, and deployment considerations for effective voice interfaces.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 针对语音识别作为机器人最自然交互方式但常依赖在线API的现状，本文综述了将本地化ASR模型集成到智能机器人的各种方法。文章梳理了从传统方法到Whisper等深度学习模型的演进，列举了大规模数据集和开源工具包，并按模型族、部署策略组织内容。该综述为机器人语音交互界面设计提供了全面的技术参考。

quick_skim.title: GigaChat Audio: Time-aware Large Audio Language Model
quick_skim.abstract: Temporal grounding in long recordings remains challenging for audio-conditioned LLMs. We present a time-aware audio LLM that answers questions with explicit timestamps over up to 120 minutes of input. Our approach interleaves periodic time markers with continuous audio tokens using large-scale synthetic supervision from a cascaded pipeline. Our model achieves strong temporal-grounding accuracy on short and long benchmarks and supports time-anchored fragment descriptions and summaries. Extensive ablations examine how time representation, marker frequency, tokenization, and duration-mixture design affect accuracy and computational cost. We release model weights and datasets to support further research on time-aware audio understanding, available at https://huggingface.co/ai-sage/GigaChat3.1-Audio-10B-A1.8B.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: Time-aware audio LLM for long recordings, enabling timestamped question answering
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 面向长录音的时间感知音频大语言模型，支持时间戳问答
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 面向长录音的时间感知音频大语言模型，支持时间戳问答
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 面向长录音的时间感知音频大语言模型，支持时间戳问答
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: Presents GigaChat Audio, a time-aware audio LLM that answers questions with explicit timestamps for up to 120-minute inputs, achieved by interleaving time markers with audio tokens and training on synthetic data, demonstrating strong temporal grounding.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 针对音频条件下大语言模型难以处理长录音时间定位的挑战，本文提出一种时间感知音频LLM。通过将周期性时间标记与连续音频token交错编码，并利用基于级联流水线的大规模合成监督进行训练，模型在短时和长时基准上取得了优越的时间定位精度，支持时间锚定的片段描述与摘要。该工作为构建能够理解长音频并执行时间相关问答的语音大模型贡献了关键技术，代码与权重开源。
quick_skim.llm_tldr: 针对音频条件下大语言模型难以处理长录音时间定位的挑战，本文提出一种时间感知音频LLM。通过将周期性时间标记与连续音频token交错编码，并利用基于级联流水线的大规模合成监督进行训练，模型在短时和长时基准上取得了优越的时间定位精度，支持时间锚定的片段描述与摘要。该工作为构建能够理解长音频并执行时间相关问答的语音大模型贡献了关键技术，代码与权重开源。
quick_skim.matched_query_text: Papers central to 语音识别、合成、交互、语音大模型、多模态大模型, especially work that connects or combines: automatic speech recognition accuracy improvement techniques; text to speech quality enhancement methods; voice user interface conversational AI design; large-scale self-supervised speech model training; vision and speech multimodal learning; data pipeline and preprocessing for speech model training; advanced training methodologies for large speech models; techniques to enhance speech recognition performance and accuracy; efficiency improvements for speech synthesis and inference speed.

quick_skim.title: Data Augmentation for L2 English Speaking Assessment using TTS
quick_skim.abstract: Automated assessment of second language (L2) speaking proficiency relies on large-scale annotated speech data, which remains scarce compared to widely available written learner corpora. A promising direction for addressing this imbalance is to use text-to-speech (TTS) and voice cloning to convert written L2 production into synthetic speech. However, written and spoken L2 differ fundamentally: spontaneous speech includes disfluencies and discourse markers, while writing is more planned and complex. This raises the question of what is required to generate synthetic L2 speech suitable for assessment. We address this through a systematic analysis of speaker-text relationships using COREFL, a publicly available corpus containing paired spoken and written responses from the same L2 learners to the same questions across modalities. In our proposed framework, we first address the structural differences between written and spoken language by transforming written responses into spoken-style transcripts ("speechification") using a large language model. These transcripts are then converted into speech using a TTS/voice-cloning model. To assign a voice to each synthetic response, we investigate different speaker-text pairing strategies based on shared learner attributes (proficiency level, first language, both, or neither). We evaluate our data augmentation techniques on the language assessment task, with improvements shown in both wav2vec2 (audio-based) and ModernBERT (text-based) scoring systems. Results show that matching speakers and texts by proficiency level yields the most robust synthetic speech. Moreover, raw written text leads to a strong mismatch with spoken language, while speechification substantially reduces this gap and improves grading performance.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: uses TTS to generate synthetic L2 speech from written corpora for data augmentation in speaking assessment
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 利用TTS将书面语料转化为合成L2语音，为口语评估增强数据
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 利用TTS将书面语料转化为合成L2语音，为口语评估增强数据
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 利用TTS将书面语料转化为合成L2语音，为口语评估增强数据
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: Explores data augmentation for L2 English speaking assessment by converting written learner corpora to synthetic speech via TTS, addressing the scarcity of annotated spoken L2 data.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 针对L2口语评估中标注语音数据稀缺的问题，提出使用TTS和声音克隆将书面语料转化为合成语音的数据增强方法。通过COREFL语料库系统分析了书面与口语差异对合成数据质量的影响，确定了生成适用评估数据的条件。该工作为语音模型训练中的数据预处理和管线构建提供了可行方案，尤其适用于口语评估任务。
quick_skim.llm_tldr: 针对L2口语评估中标注语音数据稀缺的问题，提出使用TTS和声音克隆将书面语料转化为合成语音的数据增强方法。通过COREFL语料库系统分析了书面与口语差异对合成数据质量的影响，确定了生成适用评估数据的条件。该工作为语音模型训练中的数据预处理和管线构建提供了可行方案，尤其适用于口语评估任务。

quick_skim.title: Encoder-Side Neuron Identification and Amplification for Acoustic Perception in Large Audio-Language Models
quick_skim.abstract: Large audio-language models (LALMs) often underperform on fine-grained, non-semantic attributes of speech, such as a speaker's emotion, despite strong performance on speech content. Improving this without the cost of retraining calls for an effective inference-time intervention, yet most existing methods intervene only after the audio encoder and operate at a relatively coarse granularity. The encoder itself, where acoustic information is first extracted from the waveform, remains largely unexplored, especially at the level of individual neurons. We introduce IAAN, Identifying and Amplifying Acoustic Neurons, a training-free and label-free method that scores each feed-forward neuron in the audio encoder by contrasting its activation on the real waveform with that on a noise reference lacking the real audio's acoustic information. IAAN then amplifies a small set of the highest-scoring neurons at inference. Across ten non-semantic speech attributes, IAAN improves average accuracy by 25.7 points on Audio-Flamingo-3, 21.4 on Qwen2.5-Omni, and 9.7 on Kimi-Audio. It also improves a model already explicitly fine-tuned to prioritize acoustic evidence. In controlled comparisons, both the encoder locus and neuron-level selectivity prove necessary for this gain. Intervening after the encoder, at the decoding side or inside the language model, yields little to no improvement, or even deteriorates accuracy. The improvement also depends on which specific neurons are amplified, not merely on their number, confirming that IAAN's acoustic score succeeds in identifying the neurons that matter. These results show that a small, precisely targeted intervention inside the audio encoder is an effective and largely untapped way to strengthen the acoustic understanding of LALMs, opening a new direction for inference-time methods that improve acoustic perception through neuron-level access to the encoder.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: Inference-time method to enhance acoustic perception in large audio-language models
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 推理时增强大音频语言模型声学感知的方法
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 推理时增强大音频语言模型声学感知的方法
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 推理时增强大音频语言模型声学感知的方法
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: Introduces IAAN, a training-free and label-free method that identifies and amplifies acoustic neurons in the audio encoder to improve fine-grained speech attribute (e.g., emotion) perception in large audio-language models.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 针对大音频语言模型在细粒度声学属性（如情绪）感知上的不足，提出训练无关且无标签的IAAN方法，通过对比语音与噪声激活来识别并放大编码器中与声学感知相关的神经元，在推理时提升模型对非语义信息的捕获能力，为增强语音大模型感知提供新途径。
quick_skim.llm_tldr: 针对大音频语言模型在细粒度声学属性（如情绪）感知上的不足，提出训练无关且无标签的IAAN方法，通过对比语音与噪声激活来识别并放大编码器中与声学感知相关的神经元，在推理时提升模型对非语义信息的捕获能力，为增强语音大模型感知提供新途径。
